title: I built Tiktok for Trading Strategies
author: iamnottravis
selftext:
Built this initially for myself. Kept on polishing (which took longer than expected) and now releasing it to public.

  
AI finds strategies -> I backtest them -> We show the live setups as tiktok-style feed

Works for US Equities + Futures + Crypto.

Looking for feedback and users🤞

comments.author: Tripple_sneeed
comments.body:
I don’t immediately discount it because it’s vibecoded, but I treat it with extreme skepticism. 

I actually downloaded to see what it does. No one is making an account for this lmao, especially when you try to bait and switch by taking me through 10 screens before you lock me out at account creation. 

Show what the app does without an account and maybe you will gain some users

replies.author: iamnottravis
replies.body:
The app ain't vibecoded, but yeah, of course we used a lot of AI to code. How else do you do it in 2026.

We've taken real care with the experience: the charts are fully interactive, the feed took ages to make smooth, and everything is real-time. Screeners run every minute, custom alerts on price, volume and other indicators, and we've carefully backtested and evaluated a lot of strategies.  


\> Show what the app does without an account

There's literally a video right at the start of onboarding. And me sharing pieces like this in public is showing what the app does. Sorry you felt baited, that wasn't the intent. I just didn't want to maintain a separate logged-out experience. I'd rather optimise for users who actually find value in it, and will try to do a better job to show what's in it before the user is asked to sign in.

comments.author: This_Significance_65
comments.body:
AI finds unusable/not optimal strategies.
If it worked, you wouldn’t find it by vibe coding it.

replies.author: iamnottravis
replies.body: Most of what we ship are public technical setups (MACD cross, Bollinger breakout, RSI divergence). They've been known for decades and still work if you scan a wide enough universe and apply them with discipline. AI just helps surface candidates faster, the edge is the curation and showing actual win rate on every card.

comments.author: Either_Door_5500
comments.body:
The mobile first feed for strategies is an interesting concept because it makes complex quantitative data a lot easier to digest quickly. One thing to keep in mind when backtesting US equities for a public feed is how you handle restatements and corporate actions over time. If your background engine is just pulling current historical data, your backtests might suffer from look ahead bias because companies frequently amend their old 10-Q and 10-K filings months after the fact.

I have been working on an api in this space - it helps with structured and LLM friendly SEC data for devs, which might be useful for ensuring the equity strategies your AI finds are backtested against the actual point-in-time financial facts.

Let me know if it sounds relevant.

replies.author: iamnottravis
replies.body: sounds cool mate. but I dont use the public feed - 10Q/10K - but only the price, volume and other indicators. Technical analysis
